qualification.education_qualification: Advocate with not less than 7 years practice in Tamil Nadu Courts, Advocate with not less than 10 years practice in Tamil Nadu Courts for Special Public Prosecutor posts
qualification.details: Public Prosecutor: Advocate with not less than 7 years practice in Tamil Nadu Courts. Government Pleader: Advocate with not less than 7 years practice in Tamil Nadu Courts. Additional Government Pleader Cum Additional Public Prosecutor: Advocate with not less than 7 years practice in Tamil Nadu Courts. Special Public Prosecutor: Advocate with not less than 10 years practice in Tamil Nadu Courts. Pleader doing Government Work: Advocate with not less than 7 years practice in Tamil Nadu Courts. Additional Government Pleader: Advocate with not less than 7 years practice in Tamil Nadu Courts. Special Public Prosecutor for SC&ST (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989: Advocate with not less than 10 years practice in Tamil Nadu Courts. Special Public Prosecutor for Human Rights Act, 1933: Advocate with not less than 10 years practice in Tamil Nadu Courts.

how_to_apply: Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Download the application form from the official website: .
Fill the application form carefully with correct information.
Attach self-attested copies of required documents such as photograph, signature, ID proof, and certificates.
Check all details carefully before submission.
Send the completed application form to the address mentioned in the notification through Speed Post / Registered Post or as instructed.
Ensure the application reaches before the last date: 29-06-2026.
Keep a photocopy of the submitted application form for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the Ariyalur District Court Law Officer posts?
faq.answer: The last date to receive the application for the Ariyalur District Court Law Officer recruitment is 29th June 2026. Ensure your application reaches the designated address before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available for Law Officer posts?
faq.answer: There are a total of 15 vacancies announced for various Law Officer positions within the Ariyalur District Court for this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q3. What is the application mode for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The application process for the Ariyalur District Court Law Officer recruitment is strictly offline. Candidates need to download the form and submit it via post.

faq.question: Q4. What are the minimum practice years required for the Law Officer posts?
faq.answer: For most Law Officer posts, advocates need to have at least 7 years of practice. However, for Special Public Prosecutor roles, a minimum of 10 years of practice is required.

faq.question: Q5. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, as per the provided data, there is no application fee mentioned for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for this recruitment.

faq.question: Q6. What is the official website of Ariyalur District Court?
faq.answer: The official website for Ariyalur District Court is not provided in the extracted data. Candidates are advised to search for the official portal to get the most accurate information.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for these posts?
faq.answer: The selection process involves an initial application submission followed by the preparation of a panel by the Government. Specific details might be available in the official notification.

faq.question: Q8. What is the job type for these Law Officer positions?
faq.answer: The Law Officer positions announced by the Ariyalur District Court are on a contractual basis.

faq.question: Q9. Can candidates from outside Tamil Nadu apply?
faq.answer: The eligibility criteria specify practice in Tamil Nadu Courts, suggesting that candidates should have relevant experience within the state's legal system.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I find the application form?
faq.answer: The application form can be downloaded from the official website of the Ariyalur District Court. Candidates should refer to the "How to Apply" section for more details.
